step_analysis.summary: One main elongated frame member is assembled/associated with four similar upright post/leg members. The posts are shown separately but as part of the active assembly context around the large frame. This step most plausibly creates a frame-side subassembly consisting of the main base/frame plus four uprights.

A second analogous assembly is formed: a long side rail/frame member together with four similar upright post/leg members. Geometry parallels step 0 but colors differ and exact internal identity should not be cross-tracked by color.

A previously built large frame appears as a black-box component. Two wheel-related modules are actively attached: one larger two-wheel/axle module at one end and one smaller roller/caster-like bracket at the other end. This yields a mobile support frame.
Final assembly joins the large body/bin shell to the completed wheeled support frame from the previous step, producing the finished product.
